title: Pedilonum putnamii
taxonomy: Pedilonum putnamii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to India and has been classified within the genus Pedilonum. The classification of Pedilonum putnamii places it within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. These angiosperms are further divided into the clade called eudicots. The exact classification of Pedilonum putnamii can be refined to kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, and clade Eudicots.

intro: Pedilonum putnamii, also known as the Philippine logwood, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Philippines, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pedilonum putnamii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Pedilonum putnamii is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in the form of a topical ointment or a tea to leverage its health benefits.
